name: Nebraska (United States)
description: A blue field charged in the center with the Great Seal of Nebraska in gold.
history: Nebraska was the last of the 48 contiguous states to adopt an official flag. The state banner, showing the seal in gold and silver on a field of national blue, was authorized by the legislature in 1925; it was not formally designated as the state flag until 1963.

features.role: the Great Seal of the State of Nebraska
features.symbolism: depicts a steamboat on the Missouri River, a settler's cabin and sheaves of wheat representing agriculture, a blacksmith with hammer and anvil representing the mechanic arts, and a train heading toward the Rocky Mountains; encircled by the state motto 'EQUALITY BEFORE THE LAW'
